title: Almond Bliss Sickness Soup
description: A buttery, crunchy Thai-inspired dish for sick days featuring flaked almonds, cooked with a blend of plant-based ingredients and served as a comforting soup.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine almond flour, nutritional yeast, garlic powder, dashi paste, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Gradually add water while stirring constantly until the mixture forms a thick paste-like consistency. Add flaked almonds and oil or coconut oil. Stir to ensure all ingredients are evenly distributed.

Transfer the mixture into a lightly greased pressure cooker (or a non-stick pot for slow cooking). Cook on high pressure for 10 minutes for a manual pressure cooker, or follow your appliance's instructions for a smart pressure cooker.
Let the pressure release naturally for about 5 minutes before opening. Stir gently to ensure everything is evenly heated and then serve hot with extra flaked almonds on top as garnish.
